name: Gallery
description: Sealed-submission art gallery starbridge-app: artists COMMIT a sealed (hashed) submission during a SUBMISSION phase, the curator CLOSES submissions, artists REVEAL their pieces, and the curator CURATES (features a piece for display). The commit phase is ON-LEDGER — a gallery is a factory-born sovereign cell whose installed CellProgram IS the curation policy: each submission slot is WriteOnce (a sealed submission is FROZEN the instant committed, so a piece cannot be swapped out from under the curator after the call closes; the anti-tamper tooth, an EXECUTOR REFUSAL) and the PHASE is anti-rollback (Monotonic floor + StrictMonotonic advance on close_submissions/curate). Folds onto the sealed-auction commit-reveal pattern: a committed submission opens to exactly its piece (under collision-resistance), no reveal before the call closes, and a non-submitted artist can never be featured.
state_constraints: WriteOnce(SUBMIT_BASE + i)  // the anti-tamper submission board — each committed sealed submission is frozen
Monotonic(PHASE_SLOT)  // the anti-rollback floor (the phase may never rewind), on every method
MethodIs(close_submissions) => StrictMonotonic(PHASE_SLOT)  // SUBMISSION -> REVEAL, strictly advancing
MethodIs(curate) => StrictMonotonic(PHASE_SLOT)  // REVEAL -> CURATED, strictly advancing

deos.mount: register_deos (src/lib.rs) — folds the composed DeosApp into the StarbridgeAppContext affordance registry and SEEDS the gallery cell (installs the gallery program, PHASE = SUBMISSION); DeosApp::mount yields the axum router
deos.publish: Signature (the gallery cell is a dregg:// sturdyref a visitor/auditor on another federation reacquires)
deos.on_ledger_submission_board: THE PAYOFF — the commit phase is ON-LEDGER: a WriteOnce submission-board cell (slots SUBMIT_BASE..STATE_SLOTS). A sealed submission is FROZEN the instant it is committed, so SWAPPING a committed piece is an EXECUTOR REFUSAL (WriteOnce), not an in-process membership check. This is the anti-tamper guarantee, lifted from a Rust check into the verified executor.

affordances.submit: GATED (cap∧state), Either — SUBMISSION precondition (PHASE==SUBMISSION); the fire writes the next free WriteOnce submission slot (read from live state), the executor re-enforcing the WriteOnce board (swapping a committed submission is REFUSED)
affordances.close_submissions: GATED (cap∧state), None/root — SUBMISSION precondition; advances PHASE SUBMISSION -> REVEAL, the executor re-enforcing StrictMonotonic(PHASE)
affordances.reveal: GATED (cap∧state), Either — REVEAL precondition (PHASE==REVEAL); records the revealed piece
affordances.curate: GATED (cap∧state), None/root — REVEAL precondition; advances PHASE REVEAL -> CURATED and writes FEATURED / FEATURED_HASH (frozen by WriteOnce), the executor re-enforcing StrictMonotonic(PHASE)

deos.rights_ladder: Signature ⊂ Either ⊂ None IS the visitor ⊂ artist ⊂ curator ladder
deos.seam: CLOSED — the fire runs against the seeded gallery cell carrying the full gallery program (the WriteOnce submission board + result registers + the Monotonic/StrictMonotonic PHASE lifecycle), so SWAPPING a committed sealed submission (WriteOnce) and a PHASE that rewinds / does-not-advance (StrictMonotonic) BITE in the deos fire path itself (tests/deos_seam.rs). The factory-born cell carries the flat state_constraints predicate, so the anti-tamper and anti-rollback teeth are executor refusals on the born cell too (tests/factory_birth.rs).
